mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment rate below 4.5% signals tight labor market conditions that drive corporate profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 4.5%, or 60d cumulative returns in these periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates stronger corporate output capacity, which correlates with positive equity returns as investors price in future earnings growth over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO rises by >1% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand, which drives equity valuations higher through improved corporate revenue prospects over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS rise by >2% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPI inflation above 3% triggers expectations of monetary tightening, which depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 3%, or 60d cumulative returns in these periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 150k new jobs signals weak labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are positive in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS < 150k, or 5d cumulative returns in these periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in GDP (GDPC1) below 2% growth signals weak aggregate demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns are positive in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 < 2%, or 60d cumulative returns in these periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in industrial production (INDPRO) and rise in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates economic contraction, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through deteriorating corporate fundamentals.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are positive in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO falls by >1% and UNRATE rises by >0.3pp month-over-month.
